curriculumData: 5. Amino acid and Protein chemistry and metabolism

qnaData.stem: Describe the structure of biological membranes. Discuss the various transport mechanisms across membranes with suitable examples. Explain the rationale behind giving Oral Rehydration Salt (ORS) therapy to patients with diarrhoea. [3+4+3=10]
SAQ: What are essential fatty acids? Briefly discuss the functions of essential fatty acids. Name a deficiency disorder arising due to lack of them. [2+2+1=5]
SAQ: What is the isoelectric point of amino acids? Enumerate the methods of protein precipitation. [2+3=5]
qnaData.chapter: 5. Amino acid and Protein chemistry and metabolism

qnaData.stem: SAQ: How ammonia is transported in the body? Explain the biochemical basis of ammonia toxicity. [3+2=5]
qnaData.chapter: 5. Amino acid and Protein chemistry and metabolism

qnaData.stem: Discuss the role of folic acid in one-carbon metabolism. Add a note on the metabolic link between B12 and folic acid. [6+4=10]
Explain the chemiosmotic theory of oxidative phosphorylation with a neat diagram. How does NADH produced in the glycolysis pathway get oxidized by the respiratory chain? [5+5=10]
SAQ: Briefly discuss the mechanism of action of steroid hormones. [5]
Discuss the causes, diagnosis, and management of phenylketonuria. Name the biologically important substances produced from tryptophan. [8+2=10]
qnaData.chapter: 5. Amino acid and Protein chemistry and metabolism

qnaData.stem: SAQ: Explain the reasoning behind: Impairment of hexose monophosphate (HMP) shunt pathway leads to haemolytic anaemia. [5]
SAQ: Explain the reasoning behind: Carnitine deficiency in newborns can cause hypoglycemia. [5]
SAQ: Explain the reasoning behind: Brown adipose tissue is the site of 'non-shivering thermogenesis'. [5]
SAQ: Explain the reasoning behind: Fructose is more atherogenic and diabetogenic than glucose. [5]

Compare and contrast the oxygen binding properties of haemoglobin and myoglobin using appropriate graphs. How does this relate to their physiological roles? Explain the rationale by which haemoglobin can efficiently load as well as unload oxygen. [3+3+4=10]

a) Describe enzyme inhibition in the context of methanol poisoning. Which enzyme is targeted for therapy and why? [5]
b) What are iso-enzymes? Discuss their clinical significance with suitable examples. [5]
SAQ: What is free radical injury and what are the role of antioxidants in preventing such injury? [5]
SAQ: What is the principle of PCR? Name two applications where PCR is used. [5]
SAQ: What are restriction endonucleases? Briefly discuss their role in “Recombinant DNA Technology”. [5]
SAQ: Describe the structure of antibody with a labelled diagram. Discuss in brief, four broad categories of antibody effector functions. [5]

a) What are live, attenuated vaccines? How are they useful in preventing disease? [5]
b) Differentiate between active and passive immunity. Give ONE example each of natural and artificial passive immunity. [5]
SAQ: Discuss the derangements of various analytes in serum and urine in different types of jaundice (pre-hepatic, hepatic and post-hepatic) in a tabular form. [7]
SAQ: Explain with the help of a neat labelled diagram, how iron is absorbed from the intestinal lumen into the blood stream. [5]
SAQ: Enumerate the mechanisms involved in the maintenance of acid-base balance in the human body. Briefly describe ANY ONE of them. [5]
SAQ: Write short notes on: Tumor suppressor genes. [4]
SAQ: Write short notes on: Post-transcriptional processing of mRNA. [4]

Describe the various biochemical changes in carbohydrate metabolism during starvation. Discuss the role of fructose 2,6-bisphosphate in the reciprocal regulation of glycolysis and gluconeogenesis. [6+4=10 marks]
Discuss the regulation of cholesterol biosynthesis. Briefly describe the role of dietary control and drugs in managing hypercholesterolemia. [5+5=10 marks]
SAQ: Explain the biochemical basis of why a person suffering from scurvy is likely to develop characteristic symptoms like spongy and bleeding gums. [5 marks]
With the help of a diagram, explain the electron transport chain. How does the transport of electrons lead to the generation of ATP? [5+5=10 marks]

(a) List the steps involved in the synthesis of thyroid hormones. Discuss the role of Thyroid Function Tests in diagnosing hypothyroidism. [2+3=5 marks]
(b) Briefly discuss the role of cAMP as a second messenger. [5 marks]
SAQ: Explain the biochemical basis of why a person suffering from G6PD deficiency develops hematuria after consuming an antimalarial drug like Primaquine. [5 marks]
Describe the fluid mosaic model of the plasma membrane; use a diagram to support your description. How is membrane fluidity maintained? [5+5=10 marks]
How is ammonia transported from different parts of the body to the liver for urea synthesis? Briefly describe the steps in how the liver can detoxify the ammonia. [5+5=10 marks]

qnaData.stem: SAQ: Explain the biochemical basis of why a diet deficient in Tryptophan can result in Pellagra. [5 marks]

SAQ: (a) Enumerate the DNA repair mechanisms. Discuss briefly about one such mechanism and a disease associated with its dysfunction. [2+3=5 marks]
(b) Discuss various post-transcriptional modifications that a hnRNA undergoes to become a mature mRNA. [5 marks]
SAQ: (c) Describe various mechanisms involved (with one example each) in the conversion of proto-oncogenes to oncogenes. [5 marks]

A 5 year girl child was brought to the Emergency Room (ER) with complaints of severe abdominal pain. Laboratory investigations revealed anemia and sickle-shaped cells in peripheral blood film. Gel electrophoresis for Hb revealed HbS and HbF bands. Based on the clinical scenario, answer the following questions:
(a) Discuss the biochemical basis of severe abdominal pain in patients of sickle cell anemia.
(b) What is HbF and why was its band observed on electrophoresis in this case? [2+2=4 marks]
SAQ: (c) Describe how RFLP is used to diagnose this condition (Sickle Cell Anemia). [4 marks]
VSAQ: (d) Explain the principle of electrophoresis. [2 marks]

(a) What are sub-unit vaccines? Discuss their advantages and disadvantages. [5 marks]
(b) Describe the various biological effector functions mediated by complement activation. Illustrate with the help of a diagram. [5 marks]

SAQ: (b) Discuss the importance of dietary fibers. [5 marks]
SAQ: Differentiate between endogenous and exogenous antigen processing and presentation. [5 marks]

SAQ: (a) Explain the role of Cyt P450 in the detoxification of drugs. [5 marks]
SAQ: (b) List two important functions of albumin. Discuss the clinical importance of measuring the albumin / globulin (A/G) ratio. [2+3=5 marks]
